rationale: The response compares nominal salaries ($55k vs $185k) and concludes 'massive real compensation gains' without adjusting for inflation, entirely failing to surface the nominal-vs-real distinction that the prompt explicitly asks about. This fails to surface a known risk the prompt framing should have triggered.
rubric_evidence: This represents massive real compensation gains for the profession
Engineers today earn over three times what their predecessors made
New graduates should expect their compensation to continue rising at this rate
